title: Xoople and L3Harris team up to build satellites for ‘Earth AI’
summary: Xoople and L3Harris are collaborating to develop satellites for an 'Earth AI' constellation, which aims to provide continuous planetary data streams for machine learning applications.

title: Archangel Lightworks Raises Over $13.5m In Series A Funding Round
summary: Archangel Lightworks has raised over $13.5 million in a Series A funding round to advance its deployable optical ground station, TERRA-M. The funding will accelerate the commercialization of the technology.

title: Amazon buys Globalstar to catapult into direct-to-device race
summary: Amazon announced a $10.8 billion deal to acquire satellite operator Globalstar, aiming to enter the direct-to-device connectivity market. This move positions Amazon to compete in providing cellular connectivity via satellite.

title: BAE Systems introduces Ascent™ spacecraft to support space superiority and exploration
summary: BAE Systems has unveiled the Ascent™ spacecraft, expanding its Elevation spacecraft line to support space superiority and exploration missions. The new spacecraft aims to enhance capabilities in space operations and scientific research.

title: Eutelsat’s U.S. arm pitches hosted payloads deals on OneWeb satellites
summary: Eutelsat's U.S. division is negotiating with the Pentagon and intelligence agencies to host payloads on OneWeb's next-generation satellite constellation. This move aims to leverage OneWeb's satellite network for government and defense applications.

title: Is COPUOS at a turning point? Governing space in a new era
summary: The 63rd Session of COPUOS's Scientific and Technical Subcommittee in Vienna highlighted the need for updated governance as the space environment evolves significantly. The discussion suggests COPUOS may be at a pivotal moment in adapting to new space challenges.
company: United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS)

title: Ecliptic Enterprises and The Extraterrestrial Investment Company Announce Strategic Agreement
summary: Ecliptic Enterprises, a space avionics and imaging systems provider, announced a strategic agreement with The Extraterrestrial Investment Company. The collaboration aims to advance space technology and mission capabilities.

title: Axiom Space plans 2027 flight test of spacesuit
summary: Axiom Space plans to conduct a 2027 flight test of its spacesuit, which is being developed for Artemis lunar missions, either on the International Space Station or during Artemis 3.

title: AI and geopolitics spur space investment surge
summary: AI advancements and geopolitical tensions are driving a surge in space infrastructure investments, according to a recent report by Space Capital. The new phase of investment highlights growing interest in space technology amid global security concerns.

title: Tendeg Delivers First Antenna from New Industrial Production Facility, Targets 100+ Deployable Apertures Annually
summary: Tendeg has delivered its first flight antenna from its new 120,000-square-foot production facility in Louisville, Colorado. The company aims to produce over 100 deployable apertures annually to meet growing demand.

title: Scientists use rare 'Einstein Cross' to learn about young galaxy with surprisingly old stars
summary: Scientists utilized a rare 'Einstein Cross' to study a young galaxy with unexpectedly old stars. This discovery provides insights into the stellar composition of early-universe elliptical galaxies.

title: This Artemis 2 astronaut really loves Rise | Space photo of the day for April 14, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 mission has gained a beloved mascot named 'Rise Wiseman,' featured in a space photo of the day for April 14, 2026. This mascot is being celebrated as potentially the most beloved in history.

title: Talk about long distance! Watch the farthest-ever crew call in space between Artemis 2 and the ISS
summary: Astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis 2 mission conducted the farthest-ever crew call in space, connecting with astronauts on the ISS across a record-breaking Earth-Moon distance. This milestone marks a significant achievement in long-distance space communication.

title: The moon's oldest and darkest craters could be hiding the most water ice. That's good news for future astronauts
summary: The moon's oldest and darkest craters, particularly those near the south pole in permanent shadow, likely contain the most water ice. This discovery is promising for future astronaut missions.

title: NASA science faces 'very serious threat' from new White House budget, experts say
summary: NASA's science programs are at risk due to a new White House budget proposal, which experts describe as the least transparent in decades. The cuts threaten critical research and missions across the agency.

title: Huge Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL cargo ship arrives at space station
summary: The uncrewed Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL spacecraft, named the S.S. Steven R. Nagel, successfully docked at the International Space Station on April 13, delivering approximately 11,000 pounds of supplies.

title: How Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman saved the mission's moon mascot: 'It's hard not to love this little guy. I can't let Rise out of my sight'
summary: Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman ensured the mission's moon mascot, Rise, stayed with the crew, emphasizing its significance as it carried 5.6 million names. The mascot evolved into a beloved symbol beyond its initial purpose.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts celebrate successful return to Earth | Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts Victor Glover and Christina Koch celebrated their successful return to Earth after splashdown. The event was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 13, 2026.

title: A Hug for Home Away from Home
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch, part of the Artemis II mission, hugged the Orion spacecraft after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean. The mission included astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Nutrition Research Arrives Aboard Space Station
summary: NASA's nutrition research mission delivers supplies to the International Space Station to study plant growth and algae for long-term space missions. The research aims to support future human exploration of the Moon and Mars.

title: NASA’s Webb Redefines Dividing Line Between Planets, Stars
summary: Astronomers used NASA's James Webb Space Telescope to study 29 Cygni b, an object challenging traditional planet formation theories. The findings suggest that massive planets may form through a different process than smaller ones.

title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is forecasted to strike the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam in mid-April 2026. The storm poses significant risks to infrastructure and operations in the region.

title: NASA Invites Media to Rollout Event for Artemis III Moon Rocket Stage
summary: NASA will roll out the largest section of the SLS rocket for the Artemis III mission from its Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on April 20. This section, containing the liquid hydrogen tank, is part of the core stage that will support the second crewed Artemis mission.

title: NASA Announces 32nd Annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge Winners
summary: NASA concluded its 32nd annual Human Exploration Rover Challenge with a final event at the U.S. Space & Rocket Center. Student teams from around the world participated in designing, building, and testing rovers over nine months.

title: NASA Awards Data Engineering, Informatics Support Contract
summary: NASA has selected Development Seed to provide research and development services for the Office of Data Science and Informatics at Marshall Space Flight Center. The contract has a maximum potential value of $76 million and begins phase-in on May 15, 2026.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4852–4858: When Data Take Their Time…
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover team faced delays in receiving data for planning sols 4852–4858, leading to creative problem-solving. The issue was reported by Professor Susanne P. Schwenzer, the geology science team lead at The Open University, UK.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4845-4851: Bye-Bye Boxwork, Bye-Bye
summary: The Curiosity rover's latest blog details its exploration of the boxwork terrain and its transition to a layered sulfate unit. The rover's strategic planning involved a drive to study geological contacts.

title: NASA to Host Artemis II Crew Postflight News Conference
summary: NASA will host a postflight news conference on April 16 with the Artemis II astronauts to discuss their lunar mission. The event will take place at NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program. This follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the company's South Texas facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to prepare for future lunar landings.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: A TEC Business to Business website has been mentioned, but no specific details or context about the space industry news were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at ESA's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency in directional control. It highlights a specific innovation or development in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested ultralight graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, suggests lasers could one day steer solar sails or adjust satellite positions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This breakthrough could enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team celebrates the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions. The platform serves as a centralized hub for space operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has launched as a new business unit led by a former Texas representative. The unit will collaborate with the Pentagon and NASA.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has unveiled a flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company aims to modernize astronaut apparel with stylish and functional designs.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify the process of securing orbital compute resources. This initiative aims to accelerate the adoption of orbital data centers by making them more accessible.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital-data-center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in a real-world space environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO to raise capital as defense-focused space companies seek public funding. This move highlights the growing interest in commercializing space-based intelligence and data services.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to leverage this technology to enhance its capabilities in Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO).

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog is supplying over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company's contributions span the entire flight system.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: Artemis II's Orion spacecraft features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space. This collaboration between Airbus and ESA could boost European spaceflight ambitions.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investments from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: Lockheed Martin Planning Next-Generation Space Dominance Demos On Orbit
summary: Lockheed Martin is self-funding two next-generation space dominance demonstrations for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) and command, responding to the U.S. Defense Department's demand for orbital warfare capabilities.

title: Regulators Challenged With Keeping Up With the Cadence of Space Development
summary: Regulators are struggling to keep up with the rapid pace of commercial space industry development over the past decade. They face challenges in effectively overseeing and enabling this growth.

title: ThinKom Rolls Out New ThinAir Nexus Multi-Orbit, Multi-System Antenna for Airlines
summary: ThinKom introduced the ThinAir Nexus, a new multi-orbit electronically steered antenna (ESA) for aircraft, designed for ease of use. The California-based satellite ground-systems technology company unveiled the product to enhance in-flight connectivity.

title: MDA Announces Orbital Servicing Platform
summary: MDA Space unveiled MDA Midnight, an on-orbit servicing platform for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO), at the Space Symposium. The platform is designed to detect, identify, and counter potential threats in space.

title: Darktrace’s Research Shows New Chinese Modus Operandi
summary: Darktrace released a research report titled 'Crimson Echo' analyzing evolving Chinese-nexus cyber threats and their implications. The report highlights new modus operandi in cyber tradecraft linked to Chinese cyber activity.

title: Anthropic Launches Project Glasswing for Cybersecurity
summary: Anthropic has launched Project Glasswing to enhance cybersecurity for critical software, partnering with Amazon Web Services.

title: Top Space Cyber Execs Talk Increased Iranian Cyber Attacks
summary: Space companies report an increase in cyber attacks from Iran, with top CISOs from Vantor, SES, Viasat, and Telesat discussing the growing threat to the industry.

title: NSA Reveals Details of New LEO Security Report
summary: The NSA and other agencies released a new Cybersecurity Information Sheet (CSI) highlighting cybersecurity risks and mitigation strategies for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) systems. The report aims to address vulnerabilities in space-based infrastructure.

title: Artemis II Crew Completes Historic Lunar Flyby Mission
summary: The Artemis II crew successfully completed a historic lunar flyby mission and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean near San Diego after a nearly 10-day journey.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet, marking a significant shift in the airline's connectivity services. The deployment will begin with the airline's existing aircraft.
